gaps.reason: derived nodule path `interp.swap` has segment(s) [swap] colliding with a Mycelium reserved word ŌĆö emitting them verbatim would fail to parse (`nodule interp.swap;` tokenizes the colliding word as a keyword, not an identifier); each colliding segment is escaped with the `_kw` suffix (now `interp.swap_kw`) rather than dropped, so distinct source files whose only differing segment is a reserved word (e.g. `l1.fuse` vs `l1.nodule`) stay distinguishable instead of both collapsing onto the same nodule path (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Evaluates a `Swap` node. Implementations must be *never silent*: an out-of-domain or unsupported"] # [doc = " conversion returns an [`EvalError`] (the interpreter surfaces it), it does not coerce."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **`Sync` (M-862) + `Send` (M-864):** the parallel-pure-fragment evaluator (`crate::parallel`)"] # [doc = " shares the engine across the M-861 scheduler's worker threads (`mycelium_sched`) via `&Interpreter`"] # [doc = " ŌĆö every engine must be safely readable from multiple threads at once (`Sync`). M-864's persistent"] # [doc = " pool tightened `run_indexed` to `'static` job closures, which can no longer *borrow*"] # [doc = " `&Interpreter` from the caller's stack frame (the pool's worker threads outlive any single call);"] # [doc = " `Interpreter` is now `Clone` and stores its engine as `Arc<dyn SwapEngine>` so a job can own a"] # [doc = " cheap handle to it, which additionally requires `Send` (an `Arc<T>` is `Send` only if `T: Send +"] # [doc = " Sync`). Every shipped/known implementation (the identity engine here, the M-120 certified engines"] # [doc = " in `mycelium-cert`) is a plain, interior-mutability-free struct and satisfies both automatically;"] # [doc = " a future engine that needs interior mutability would need its own synchronization (e.g. a"] # [doc = " `Mutex`), same as any other `Send + Sync` type."] pub trait SwapEngine : Send + Sync { # [doc = " Convert `src` to `target` under `policy`, returning the converted [`Value`] or an error. The"] # [doc = " result's [`Meta`] must record `policy_used` (ADR-006) and an honest guarantee/bound."] fn swap (& self , src : & Value , target : & Repr , policy : & ContentHash) -> Result < Value , EvalError > ; }
gaps.reason: trait `SwapEngine` has supertrait bound(s) ŌĆö trait_item grammar has no supertrait syntax (`'trait' Ident type_params? '{' ...`)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The trivial swap engine: a swap whose `target` equals the source `Repr` is the identity ŌĆö exactly"] # [doc = " lossless, `guarantee` preserved. Any *cross-paradigm* swap returns"] # [doc = " [`EvalError::UnsupportedSwap`], deferring to the certified M-120 engine. This keeps M-110 honest:"] # [doc = " it executes the swaps it can prove trivially exact and refuses the rest rather than guessing."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , Default)] pub struct IdentitySwapEngine ;
gaps.reason: struct `IdentitySwapEngine` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 ŌĆö every value already copies structurally; DN-128 ┬¦6.1) ŌĆö not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: impl SwapEngine for IdentitySwapEngine { fn swap (& self , src : & Value , target : & Repr , policy : & ContentHash) -> Result < Value , EvalError > { if src . repr () != target { return Err (EvalError :: UnsupportedSwap { from : src . repr () . clone () , to : target . clone () , }) ; } let src_meta = src . meta () ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : mycelium_core :: operation_hash ("swap.identity") , inputs : vec ! [src . content_hash ()] , } , src_meta . guarantee () , src_meta . bound () . cloned () , src_meta . sparsity () , src_meta . physical () , Some (policy . clone ()) ,) . map_err (EvalError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (src . repr () . clone () , src . payload () . clone () , meta) . map_err (EvalError :: Wf) } }
